heroHeadline: Iran Silent Day Three Post-Ceasefire — FIFA Names 170 Officials, Body Cameras Debut, 61 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: Three days after the April 8 Pakistan-brokered US-Iran ceasefire removed the central obstacle to Iran's World Cup participation, Iran's government has still issued no revised statement — leaving the FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30 as the formal decision point with 61 days to the June 11 opening match. FIFA announced 170 match officials for the tournament on April 10 — the largest officiating team ever assembled, 41 more than Qatar 2022 — with match officials wearing body cameras for the first time in World Cup history. Six women officials are included; a preparation seminar is scheduled for May 31–June 10 in Miami. Iran's Group G schedule (SoFi Stadium and Lumen Field) remains unchanged with no formal withdrawal or confirmation from Tehran.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, Inside World Football
